steps.title: Identify the logical consequence
steps.content: The passage contrasts the advantage of lab studies (controlling variables) with the reality that white-throated sparrows' surroundings significantly affect their hormones. Given this contrast, the “not altogether surprising” finding would logically be what naturally follows: measurable differences between captive (lab-studied) and wild-studied sparrows.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content: Choice D directly shows this: baseline corticosterone is higher in captive sparrows than in wild sparrows. A, B, and C either misidentify the comparison, contradict the environmental significance premise, or describe within-group variation instead of between-setting differences. That matches choice D — D is correct.
